title: quercus sessiliflora var. mannifera
intro: Quercus sessiliflora var. mannifera, also known as the Quinine or Quercus, is a plant species of the beech family with unique characteristics. It is known to have medicinal properties, primarily used for its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory effects, where the Quercus has been traditionally used to treat malaria, fever, and other related health issues. The health benefits of Quercus sessiliflora var. mannifera include reducing fever, relieving body pain, and aiding in the recovery from various health conditions, making it a valuable plant for traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or infusion, where it is brewed as a tea or made into a tincture to be consumed for medicinal purposes.
